02 The 3 Ds of Documentation - YouTube.mp4
Let's face it, we all know about the importance of documenting our systems and processes, right? I mean, business consultants have been ranting about it for over a century, and whole books have been written on the importance of documenting what you do. Unfortunately, while... There are a lot of people talking about why you should document your processes. Almost no one gives a specific step-by-step system for how to document your processes well. I'm going to do just that right now. There are three steps to building out a business playbook and we call these three steps the three D's of documentation. The first step and the first D is to simply define your core process and who should own the documentation of that process. Process. Step two of the playbook documentation process is design.
In other words, we need to design your step-by-step checklists and audits and then test them in real-world scenarios to ensure that they actually work. Step three of process documentation is Deploy once we have defined and once we have designed, then we get to actually deploy the tested and proven checklist throughout the entire organization. And that's when you finally get to say goodbye to shoulder taps. Goodbye to oops, my bad. Goodbye to all the black boxes. Even goodbye to that trapped feeling that has you saying, 'Nothing ever happens unless I do it.' The three D's of documentation are all you need. So let's go through each one of them right now.
